title: When selling an EA Without Hype or Spam, what Actually Works?
author: morriase
selftext:
Assuming I’m trying to sell an EA, what actually works, and what doesn’t? What’s genuinely harmful?

I’ve seen all the usual advice: don’t spam, prove your results, join Reddit and Facebook groups, share meaningful content, try affiliates. I’ve done most of that. I hate telling people to “DM me” because it feels absurd. I don’t want to promise golden results like everyone else or drop links everywhere.

I’m not trying to sell a get-rich-quick dream. I just want organic buyers who understand how algorithms work and know they’re not magic money printers. I simply want to sell a few EAs that I personally use (and still use) to make some extra income.

The problem is that people are either extremely skeptical or looking for a system that will make them rich in a week.

For those who’ve successfully sold their algorithms in a healthy, ethical way, what did you actually do? I’m not famous and don’t have a large social media following. I’m genuinely curious what has worked for others in a sustainable, low-hype way.

comments.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
comments.body:
Same question here........

I also have my own profitable ML-based trading model for BTC (and it also works on Gold). I’ve tested it on about 5 years of data and it averaged roughly around 10% per month with fixed lot. It has a sharpe ratio of >3. I don’t have much capital myself, so I’m looking for genuine people who might be interested to buying it. If anyone’s genuinely interested, feel free to message me.

Report - [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1xwNisxVslkfPPY9g2rcbWt4shrI-9o4-/view?usp=drive\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1xwNisxVslkfPPY9g2rcbWt4shrI-9o4-/view?usp=drive_link)

comments.author: robert_lewis_12
comments.body: The only thing you need to do is show your verified MyFxBook, a robust backtest, and describe how the algo works. I would also post case studies on how your algo ran when the markets were dumping or climbing intensely. You can do this on Reddit/Facebook/LinkedIn.
